prompt: Create a seven-panel executive carousel for 'Clarity Is an Economic Instrument'. Panels: 1 title; 2 ambiguity creates translation work; 3 decision latency and rework; 4 clarity orders complexity; 5 the architecture of clarity; 6 how clarity exposes hidden friction; 7 operating principle: ambiguity is expensive, clarity creates leverage. Use qualitative diagrams only and sparse executive copy.
proof_boundaries: Do not include percentages, dollar values, or quantified ROI.
Do not imply all ambiguity is harmful or avoidable.
Present leverage as a conceptual operating relationship, not a measured result.
